items.title: Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
items.content: Intel's stock has risen a stunning 490% over the past year, a bet by Wall Street that may be running well ahead of the company's actual turnaround.

items.title: Apple to pay $250M to settle lawsuit over Siri’s delayed AI features
items.content: Apple has agreed to pay $250 million to settle a class action lawsuit for overpromising the arrival of Siri's AI features.

items.title: Apple plans to make iOS 27 a Choose Your Own Adventure of AI models
items.content: With Apple's latest operating system updates, users will reportedly have their pick of which third-party AI models they want to use for a host of tasks.

items.title: As Tim Cook steps down, Apple hit record sales — but a chip shortage looms
items.content: Cook warned that Apple is facing supply-chain headwinds from RAMageddon that could impact its business.

items.title: Microsoft’s AI data center push is colliding with its clean power goals
items.content: The push for new data centers at Microsoft is putting its key clean power goals at risk.

items.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
items.content: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models

items.title: Musk v. Altman is just getting started
items.content: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets a

items.title: Satya Nadella says he’s ready to ‘exploit’ the new OpenAI deal
items.content: Microsoft gets to offer OpenAI's tech to its cloud customers and doesn't have to pay for it. "We fully plan to exploit it," Nadella said.

items.title: How Brandeis Is Trying to Change College Shopping
items.content: A new tool on the university’s website tells you what the first year “will” cost if you get in.

items.title: Consumers Lean on a ‘Hamster Wheel’ of Credit to Manage Rising Costs
items.content: As prices increase for gas, groceries and other staples, more and more households are borrowing to get by.

items.title: Why Some People Are Allergic to ‘Peanut Butter Raises’
items.content: The beloved spread has become a metaphor for things that are less than delicious.

items.title: Sales Are Up. Celebrities Are In. Is Gap Officially Back?
items.content: Richard Dickson has drawn inspiration from the clothing retailer’s early days as he tries to regain its cultural cachet.
